title: verify-submitted output
description:
Per-job scheduler state for a just-submitted array (#157).

Answers Step 8b's question — "is every submitted job queued/running and
NOT in an error/held state?" — through a verb instead of raw
``ssh … qstat``. The caller branches on ``ok``; ``states`` carries the raw
per-job token for any case the alive/error/held classification doesn't
cover.

ok.description: True iff no submitted job is in a scheduler error or held state.
ok.title: Ok

states.description: job_id -> raw scheduler state token (e.g. SGE 'Eqw'/'r', SLURM 'RUNNING'/'PENDING').
states.title: States

healthy.description: Job ids in a healthy alive state (queued / running).
healthy.title: Healthy

error.description: Job ids in a scheduler error state (e.g. SGE Eqw).
error.title: Error

held.description: Job ids held by the scheduler.
held.title: Held

missing.description: Submitted job ids not present in the scheduler queue (already terminal/gone, or never landed).
missing.title: Missing

details.description: One-line human-readable summary the caller can surface.
details.title: Details
